# SendFast — Complete Reference

> The agentic file transfer service. Encrypted. Zero-knowledge. Built for humans and AI agents.

## Overview

SendFast is an end-to-end encrypted file transfer service designed for both humans and AI agents. Files are encrypted client-side using AES-256-GCM. The encryption key is embedded in the URL fragment (#key=...) and never transmitted to SendFast servers, ensuring true zero-knowledge architecture.

AI agents can use the SendFast REST API to securely send files (reports, exports, generated assets) to their users, or create receive links to collect files from humans for processing. This makes SendFast the file transfer layer for agentic workflows.

## How Encryption Works

1. User selects files in the browser
2. Browser generates a random AES-256-GCM encryption key
3. Each file is encrypted client-side using the Web Crypto API
4. Encrypted files are uploaded to storage
5. A shareable URL is generated: https://sendfast.app/transfer/{id}#{encryption_key}
6. The encryption key is in the URL fragment — it is never sent to the server
7. Recipient opens the link, browser extracts key from the fragment
8. Encrypted files are downloaded and decrypted client-side
9. The server never sees unencrypted data or the encryption key

## Pricing

### Free Plan ($0/forever)
- 2 GB maximum per transfer
- 7-day link expiry
- 10 transfers per month
- End-to-end encryption
- Shareable download links
- No account required

### Starter Plan ($9/month)
- 25 GB maximum per transfer
- 30-day link expiry
- 100 transfers per month
- Everything in Free, plus:
- Password protection
- Email delivery to recipients
- Download tracking and analytics
- Priority support

### Pro Plan ($19/month)
- 100 GB maximum per transfer
- 90-day link expiry
- 500 transfers per month
- Everything in Starter, plus:
- REST API access
- Custom branding on download pages
- Receive links (collect files from others)

## REST API

Base URL: https://sendfast.raicode.tech

The upload flow is a 3-step presigned-URL pattern. The server never sees plaintext files or the encryption key — agents encrypt locally, then push encrypted bytes directly to object storage via short-lived signed URLs.

Authentication is optional. Anonymous callers get free-tier limits (2 GB per transfer, 7-day expiry, daily quota). Authenticated callers get their plan's limits; pass a Better Auth session cookie on /api/upload.

### 1. Request presigned upload URLs

```
POST /api/upload
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "files": [
    { "name": "report.pdf", "size": 123456, "type": "application/pdf", "iv": "<base64url-iv>" }
  ],
  "expiry": "24h",            // "1h" | "24h" | "7d" | "30d" | "90d"
  "password": "optional",     // optional, Pro plan
  "emails": "a@x.com,b@y.com",// optional, comma-separated recipients
  "title": "optional",
  "message": "optional"
}
```

- `size` is the ORIGINAL plaintext size in bytes.
- `iv` is the 12-byte AES-GCM initialization vector used to encrypt that file, encoded as base64url.
- One `iv` per file.

Response (201):
```
{
  "transferId": "abc1234567",
  "presignedUrls": [
    { "index": 0, "url": "https://.../transfers/.../report.pdf?X-Amz-...", "key": "transfers/..." }
  ]
}
```

### 2. PUT encrypted bytes to each presigned URL

```
PUT {presignedUrls[i].url}
Content-Type: application/octet-stream
Body: <encrypted file bytes> (AES-256-GCM ciphertext || 16-byte auth tag)
```

The Content-Type must be exactly `application/octet-stream` — it is part of the signature. A non-2xx response means the upload failed; the caller must treat the transfer as invalid and retry.

### 3. Share the download link

```
https://sendfast.raicode.tech/transfer/{transferId}#{base64url-encryption-key}
```

The key lives in the URL fragment (`#`) and is never sent to the server. Only the recipient with the full link can decrypt.

### Get transfer info (public, for download)

```
GET /api/download/{transferId}
Query: ?password={password} (if password-protected)

Response: {
  id, title, message, expiresAt, createdAt, downloadCount, passwordProtected,
  files: [{ id, name, size, type, iv, url }]   // url = presigned download URL
}
```

### List owned transfers (authenticated)

```
GET /api/transfers
Cookie: <better-auth session>
Response: Transfer[]
```

### Get / delete an owned transfer (authenticated)

```
GET    /api/transfers/{id}    // owner-only, returns metadata
DELETE /api/transfers/{id}    // owner-only, deletes transfer + S3 objects
```

## Agent Integration Guide

A minimal working Node.js sender (ESM, no dependencies):

```js
import { webcrypto as crypto } from "node:crypto";
import { readFile } from "node:fs/promises";

const BASE = "https://sendfast.raicode.tech";
const b64url = (b) =>
  Buffer.from(b).toString("base64").replace(/\+/g, "-").replace(/\//g, "_").replace(/=+$/, "");

async function sendFile(path, name, type = "application/octet-stream") {
  const plaintext = await readFile(path);

  // 1. Generate AES-256 key + 96-bit IV
  const keyBytes = crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(32));
  const iv = crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(12));
  const key = await crypto.subtle.importKey("raw", keyBytes, { name: "AES-GCM" }, false, ["encrypt"]);

  // 2. Encrypt (Web Crypto output = ciphertext || 16-byte GCM tag)
  const encrypted = Buffer.from(
    await crypto.subtle.encrypt({ name: "AES-GCM", iv }, key, plaintext)
  );

  // 3. Request a presigned upload URL
  const metaRes = await fetch(`${BASE}/api/upload`, {
    method: "POST",
    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      files: [{ name, size: plaintext.length, type, iv: b64url(iv) }],
      expiry: "24h",
    }),
  });
  if (!metaRes.ok) throw new Error(`upload metadata failed: ${metaRes.status} ${await metaRes.text()}`);
  const { transferId, presignedUrls } = await metaRes.json();

  // 4. PUT encrypted bytes directly to storage
  const putRes = await fetch(presignedUrls[0].url, {
    method: "PUT",
    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/octet-stream" },
    body: encrypted,
  });
  if (!putRes.ok) throw new Error(`storage PUT failed: ${putRes.status}`);

  // 5. Return the share link (the # fragment stays client-side only)
  return `${BASE}/transfer/${transferId}#${b64url(keyBytes)}`;
}

console.log(await sendFile("./report.pdf", "report.pdf", "application/pdf"));
```

### Agent rules

- **Never send the encryption key to the server.** It only appears in the URL fragment (`#...`) or a secure side channel to the recipient.
- **Always check the PUT response.** A non-2xx on step 4 means the transfer is broken — do not hand out the link.
- **`size` is plaintext size.** Encrypted bytes are 16 bytes larger (AES-GCM tag); the server does not need to know.
- **Use base64url (not standard base64)** for the key and IV. No padding, `-`/`_` instead of `+`/`/`.
- **One IV per file, never reused.** Generate a fresh 12-byte IV for every encryption.
- **Respect plan limits** (2 GB/file, 7-day expiry, daily transfer count on free tier).

## Tech Stack

- Next.js 15 (App Router)
- TypeScript (strict mode)
- PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM
- S3-compatible object storage (MinIO)
- AES-256-GCM encryption (Web Crypto API)
- Stripe for payments
- Better Auth for authentication

## Contact

Website: https://sendfast.app
Support: support@sendfast.app
